Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Hire to Retire usually starts with posting a job ad then screening candidates conducting interviews making a hiring decision onboarding the new employee adding them to payroll and closing the case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Normally after screening you would do interviews first before making a decision because you cannot make a decision without interviewing

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In Model 1 Screen comes before both Decide and Interview and there is no edge from Interview to Decide

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That might mean that Decide can happen before Interview which would be an anomaly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The missing edge between Interview and Decide is a major issue

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In Model 2 Post is connected to both Screen and Interview directly and Interview goes to Decide then to loop onboarding then xor payroll then Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Normally you screen candidates before you conduct interviews so allowing Interview without Screen first is an anomaly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The loop on onboarding might represent that onboarding can be repeated or skipped

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The xor for payroll means either do payroll or skip so adding to payroll is not mandatory which is another anomaly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 has multiple issues incorrect order between Screen and Interview and allows skipping critical steps while Model 1 mainly has one major ordering issue

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait in Model 1 all nodes are in the strict partial order so Interview must still be executed it is not skipped but it can occur after Decide

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Model 1 all steps are required but the order between Interview and Decide is not enforced while in Model 2 onboarding and payroll can be skipped

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Being able to skip onboarding and payroll is a more severe anomaly than having all steps present but with one ordering flaw

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model 1 is closer to the normative version because it requires all steps even if the order between Interview and Decide is incorrect whereas Model 2 allows skipping critical steps
